channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Driver was on bail and probation when his 'drag racing' car hit and killed a cyclist
item.description: Lachlan Robert Carver has been sentenced to 10 years for a hit-and-run crash that killed cyclist Matthew Nilsen.
item.dc:creator: Grace Whiteside

media:group.media:description: Lachlan Carver (pictured) will spend eight years in jail for hitting and killing Matthew Nilsen.

item.title: Students receive ransom messages amid learning system hack
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of students have been unable to access schoolwork or submit assessments as access to Canvas has been paused.
item.dc:creator: Ned Hammond and Liana Walker

media:group.media:description: A ransom message received by students and teachers.

item.title: Man 'assisting detectives' with investigation into Adelaide woman's death
item.description: Police say a man is speaking to them in relation to the death of a woman whose body was found outside a block of units overlooking Henley Beach on Thursday.

media:group.media:description: Police on the Esplanade at Henley Beach where the woman's body was found.

item.title: Chinese oil tanker hit in reportedly first attack since Hormuz crisis
item.description: The chief engineer of a Chinese-owned oil tanker attacked near the Strait of Hormuz says it is "still unclear" who fired on the vessel.
item.dc:creator: Xiaoning Mo

media:group.media:description: The JV Innovation attempted to transit the Strait of Hormuz hours after joint US-Israeli military strikes on Iran but failed.

item.title: Plaintiff's lawyer describes Rebel Wilson as 'fantastical liar' in court
item.description: Final arguments in the defamation trial between Charlotte MacInnes and Rebel Wilson have heard claims of a suspicious career jump to "terrible, terrible" allegations.
item.dc:creator: Millie Roberts and Anton Rose

media:group.media:description: Actor and director Rebel Wilson (second from left) is being sued for defamation by actor Charlotte MacInnes (centre).

item.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's father won't be released from prison for sorry business
item.description: The father of Kumanjayi Little Baby will remain in prison for aggravated assault, despite calls from his lawyers for his release to attend cultural grieving for his daughter.
item.dc:creator: Elsie Lange

media:group.media:description: The court ordered Raphael Granites remain in custody while cultural grieving for his daughter takes place.

item.title: Inmate’s multiple requests for medical support unanswered, inquest hears
item.description: A Queensland prisoner died in prison of a rare bacterial condition after his calls for medical help went unanswered for weeks, a medical expert has told a coronial inquest.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Nunes-Malek and James C Taylor

media:group.media:description: The Maryborough Correctional Centre is about 100 inmates over capacity.

item.title: Why there's no end in sight for Kezie Apps, Origin's ageless wonder
item.description: Kezie Apps will be the oldest State of Origin player across both states and the men's and women's series this year, but the 35-year-old Blues stalwart shows no signs of slowing down.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: Kezie Apps will line-up for her 17th Origin match next Thursday night.

item.title: Bank hung up on pope when he tried to update his details, Chicago priest says
item.description: Father Tom McCarthy says Leo had trouble convincing his bank of his identity after being elected to lead the Catholic Church.

media:group.media:description: Leo was elected to lead the Catholic Church a year ago.

item.title: Volcanic eruption triggers mission to rescue 20 trapped hikers in Indonesia
item.description: Mount Dukono erupted on Friday morning, local time, sending plumes of ash some 10 kilometres into the sky.

media:group.media:description: Mount Dukono is one of Indonesia's most active volcanoes.

item.title: Bega's costs are up 10pc and customers will pay 'a large chunk' of it
item.description: One of Australia's biggest dairy manufacturers, Bega, is warning about soaring food inflation, as the war in the Middle East disrupts global supply chains.
item.dc:creator: Emilia Terzon

media:group.media:description: Bega warns it will need to pass on higher costs driven by the Iran war to shoppers.

item.title: Anika Wells repays $10,000 in travel costs that broke rules
item.description: An audit of Communications and Sports Minister Anika Wells's travel by the Independent Parliamentary Expenses Authority found four breaches, but cleared her controversial trips to New York and Thredbo.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong and Holly Tregenza

media:group.media:description: Sports and Communications Minister Anika Wells has repaid more than $10,000 to the taxpayer.

item.title: Melbourne men charged for allegedly sharing ISIS propaganda
item.description: The Australian Federal Police alleges the men had been "actively engaging" in sharing ISIS-inspired material, with the arrests following an investigation that began in November 2024 following a luggage check at Melbourne Airport.

media:group.media:description: The AFP's investigations began in 2024 following a luggage check at Melbourne Airport.

item.title: Work out if you are eligible for $3,600 of budget concessions, savings
item.description: Some WA families could receive thousands of dollars from this year's cost-of-living relief measures in the state budget. Here's how to work out what you are eligible for.
item.dc:creator: Cason Ho

media:group.media:description: The WA government has given everyone with a driver's licence $100 in a budget measure to ease cost-of-living pressure.

item.title: NSW Police appealing for help to solve 'suspicious' gunshot death
item.description: Police believe CCTV footage shows the final movements of the 18-year-old before he was found dead near a culvert.
item.dc:creator: Jess Scully

media:group.media:description: Police have released footage showing the last movements before a man''s body was found with a gunshot wound to the head.

item.title: Teen 'shore party' members in biggest Australian cocaine bust jailed
item.description: The pair, now aged 19, were sentenced with 18 months and two years in prison respectively.
item.dc:creator: Talissa Siganto

media:group.media:description: The teenagers will spend time behind bars for their role in the importation scheme.

item.title: Education watchdog launches new probe into 'concerning' school allegations
item.description: The Victorian education regulator is reinvestigating the elite alternative primary school at the centre of abuse allegations.
item.dc:creator: Josie Taylor and Coco Veldkamp

media:group.media:description: The Fitzroy Community School is under investigation by the Victorian Education watchdog.

item.title: Migration to be linked to housing availability under Coalition plan
item.description: The Coalition will link overseas migration to housing supply as part of its migration strategy, which would in effect slash the number of migrants allowed into the country.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Asher

media:group.media:description: Angus Taylor is looking to cap overseas migration.

item.title: Police told to switch bodycams to flight mode to avoid detection by criminals
item.description: Thousands of police officers have been advised to disable the bluetooth on their Axon body-worn cameras during raids and undercover jobs to avoid being detected and tracked, after Four Corners exposed a security flaw with the devices.
item.dc:creator: Grace Tobin

media:group.media:description: Four Corners exposed a security flaw in Axon tasers and body-worn cameras, which are used by police forces across Australia.

item.title: Ex-Sydney teacher spared jail after grooming child online
item.description: William Roberto Gulson was on Friday spared time behind bars but will be placed on the NSW Child Protection Register for eight years.
item.dc:creator: Jamie McKinnell

media:group.media:description: Former Sydney Knox Grammar teacher William Roberto Gulson was placed on the NSW Child Protection Register.

item.title: Two purged Chinese ex-defence officials sentenced to death with reprieves
item.description: Two former defence ministers have received their sentences after a long-running corruption crackdown targeting China's military.

media:group.media:description: Li Shangfu fell in Xi Jinping's anti-corruption purge that focused on the armed forces in recent years.

item.title: Trio found guilty of 'payback' murder over fake gun sale
item.description: Jury finds trio guilty of murder over the shooting death of Newcastle man Anthony Nugent over a fake gun deal in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Giselle Wakatama

media:group.media:description: Anthony Nugent was shot dead at his Hamilton South unit in September 2022.

item.title: NT government promises child protection review after child death
item.description: The NT government is set to trigger a wide-reaching review of its child protection system, in the wake of the tragic death of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.
item.dc:creator: Matt Garrick

media:group.media:description: Robyn Cahill is the minister responsible for the department that oversees child protection in the NT.

item.title: E-bike 10km/h limit in Qld should be watered down, committee says
item.description: A proposed 10km/h speed limit for e-mobility on footpaths should only apply in high pedestrian zones and not on shared paths, a Queensland parliamentary committee has recommended.
item.dc:creator: Alex Brewster and Claudia Williams

media:group.media:description: New laws have been proposed following an inquiry into e-mobility devices in Queensland.

item.title: Man jailed after burning down apartment block and running amok with axe
item.description: A man who burnt down an apartment block and terrorised a central Queensland town with an axe is jailed for six years, but will be eligible for parole in February with time already served in custody.
item.dc:creator: Liam McNally

media:group.media:description: Raymond Thomas Whyte pleaded guilty in the Mackay District Court.
